title: Generic SIEM Alert - Suspicious Encoded Command
summary: Synthetic example normalized from a generic alert export. The case models a user workstation launching an encoded command that requires analyst review.

affectedEntities.description: Synthetic user account used in the imported alert example.
Synthetic workstation that generated the process telemetry.
Reserved documentation IP used for a synthetic network observation.

evidence.title: Encoded PowerShell command observed
evidence.detail: Synthetic process event: powershell.exe launched with an encoded command argument from a user context.
evidence.analystNote: The command line needs decoding and validation before concluding malicious execution.

evidence.title: Outbound connection after script execution
evidence.detail: Synthetic network event: workstation connected to 198.51.100.77 over TCP 443 shortly after the command launched.

timeline.title: SIEM alert generated
timeline.description: Generic detection flagged suspicious encoded command execution.

timeline.title: Encoded command executed
timeline.description: PowerShell command execution was observed on the affected workstation.

timeline.title: Network connection observed
timeline.description: Outbound connection followed the suspicious command execution.

analystQuestions.question: Can the encoded command be decoded and tied to legitimate administration?
analystQuestions.rationale: The command line is suspicious, but the payload content must be reviewed before classification.

findings.title: Suspicious script execution requires validation
findings.description: A user-context encoded command was followed by outbound network activity. This is suspicious until the script content and business context are reviewed.

mitreMappings.rationale: PowerShell execution with encoded content was observed. This mapping remains analyst-authored and should be confirmed against decoded command content.

recommendations.title: Decode command and review endpoint context
recommendations.description: Decode the command, review parent process context, and confirm whether the activity is expected administration before closure.

closure.rationale: Additional evidence is required before assigning a final classification.
closure.recommendedAction: Collect decoded command content and endpoint script logs.
closure.impactSummary: Potential suspicious execution on one synthetic workstation.
